# Fate Core System — English

The complete English text of the Fate Core System SRD, included under CC-BY 3.0,
split into 12 chapters (mirroring the Traditional Chinese edition) for retrieval
and precise per-chapter citation. Text only — no trademarked logos, fonts, or dice images.

## Chapters

- [The Basics](01-basics.md)
- [Game Creation](02-game-creation.md)
- [Character Creation](03-character-creation.md)
- [Aspects & Fate Points](04-aspects-and-fate-points.md)
- [Skills & Stunts](05-skills-and-stunts.md)
- [Actions & Outcomes](06-actions-and-outcomes.md)
- [Challenges, Contests, & Conflicts](07-challenges-contests-conflicts.md)
- [Running the Game](08-running-the-game.md)
- [Scenes, Sessions, & Scenarios](09-scenes-and-scenarios.md)
- [The Long Game](10-long-term-play.md)
- [Extras](11-extras.md)
- [Veteran's Guide](12-veterans-guide.md)

Traditional Chinese edition: [`../fate-core-zh/`](../fate-core-zh/README.md).
Lightweight edition (FAE): [`../fate-accelerated-en/`](../fate-accelerated-en/README.md).

## How to use

Copy the whole `extras/fate-core-en/` folder into `game/reference/rules/` (keep only this
one complete system there), then point to it in `game/session-brief.md`. **The full
activation / replacement steps and dice notes live in [`../README.md`](../README.md)（繁體中文）.**

## License & attribution

**CC-BY 3.0.** Keep this attribution verbatim on redistribution:

> This work is based on Fate Core System and Fate Accelerated Edition (found at http://www.faterpg.com), products of Evil Hat Productions, LLC, developed, authored, and edited by Leonard Balsera, Brian Engard, Jeremy Keller, Ryan Macklin, Mike Olson, Clark Valentine, Amanda Valentine, Fred Hicks, and Rob Donoghue, and licensed for our use under the Creative Commons Attribution 3.0 Unported license.

> Fate™ and Fate Dice™ are trademarks of Evil Hat Productions, LLC.

English text sourced from the CC-BY presentation at fate-srd.com via the
brunobord/fate-srd-markdown mirror. Each chapter file carries a YAML frontmatter
block (`source`／`license`／`attribution`／`modified`) so RAG chunks built from it
inherit provenance per `protocol/RAG-PROTOCOL.md`. Full third-party
notices: [`../../THIRD-PARTY-NOTICES.md`](../../THIRD-PARTY-NOTICES.md).
